title: Choosing the Right Architecture
concept: Use MoE models for high-capacity but fast generation; use long-context models for document analysis; use multimodal models for visual tasks.

exercises.prompt: Why is it important to choose the right AI model architecture for your project?

options.text: To match the model's unique capabilities to your task's needs
Because all models cost exactly the same to run
To make sure the model only uses text and never images

exercises.explain: Choosing the right model structure (architecture) ensures your application runs efficiently, keeps costs low, and actually has the capability (like vision or long memory) to solve your specific problem.

cards.title: Right Tool, Right Job
cards.body: Would you hire a chef to fix your plumbing? Probably not! Just like people, different AI models have different superpowers. Picking the wrong type of AI model can make your app slow, expensive, or completely blind to images. Let's learn how to pick the perfect AI partner for any job!

cards.title: What is Architecture Selection?
cards.body: Choosing the right architecture means matching your project's needs to the model's superpower. Mixture of Experts (MoE) gives you massive knowledge with high speed. Long-Context models process huge documents at once. Multimodal models can see, hear, and read all at the same time.

cards.title: The AI Specialist Team
cards.body: Think of choosing a model like picking a specialist for a job. A multimodal model is like a graphic designer who understands both text and layout. A long-context model is like a legal researcher who can read a 500-page book in one sitting. An MoE model is like a giant panel of experts where only the three best specialists are called in to answer each question quickly.

cards.title: Real-World Matching
cards.body: Imagine you are building a Customer Support Bot. If the bot needs to read a 200-page product manual to answer questions, you choose a Long-Context model. If it needs to scan receipts and product photos sent by users, you choose a Multimodal model.

cards.title: The 'One Size Fits All' Myth
cards.body: Many people think that one giant AI model is perfect for every single task. In reality, using a giant general model for simple tasks is slow and expensive. Selecting specialized architectures like MoE or Multimodal saves time, money, and computing power!

cards.title: Comparing Strengths
cards.body: Let's compare the main strengths of Long-Context and Multimodal architectures so you can decide which one your project needs.

left.text: Processes massive text files, entire books, or hours of codebase files at once.

right.text: Processes and connects different formats like images, audio, video, and text.

exercises.prompt: If you need an AI that has massive general knowledge but still generates answers very quickly, which architecture is best?

options.text: A model with a very small context window
A text-only model that ignores images

exercises.explain: MoE (Mixture of Experts) models activate only a small part of their network for each prompt, allowing them to be very smart (high capacity) while remaining super fast.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A long-context model is the best choice if you need to analyze a 300-page financial report.
exercises.explain: Long-context models are specifically designed to hold thousands of pages of text in their active memory, making them perfect for analyzing entire books.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the statement about model types.
exercises.sentence: To build an app that analyzes security camera footage and describes it in text, you should use a [blank] model.
exercises.explain: Multimodal models are specially designed to accept different types of input data, such as images, audio, and text, at the same time.

exercises.prompt: Identify which of these AI models is a Multimodal model.

options.text: AI Model A: It can read 1,000 pages of text in under three seconds.
AI Model B: It routes queries to different text experts to answer fast.
AI Model C: It can look at a photo of a broken bicycle and explain how to fix it.

exercises.explain: AI Model C is a classic Multimodal model because it is the only one capable of processing and understanding visual image data alongside text instructions.

exercises.prompt: You are building an AI assistant that needs to read a student's handwritten geometry homework, look at the drawn shapes, and grade the math steps. Which model architecture should you choose?

options.text: An MoE model to route queries to speed specialists
A multimodal model to read both the text and the chart images
A long-context model to handle the document size
A text-only tiny model to save on computational costs

exercises.explain: For deep visual analysis combined with complex math reasoning, a multimodal model is the only choice that can process both the chart images and the text instructions together. MoE handles speed/capacity, and long-context handles massive text files.
